briefdescription.para: SFINAE helper for conditional template instantiation.

detaileddescription.para: SFINAE stands for "Substitution Failure Is Not An Error". It's a C++ template metaprogramming technique that allows the compiler to select different template implementations based on type properties at compile time.

When the compiler encounters template code, it tries to substitute template parameters. If substitution fails (e.g., accessing a non-existent type member), the compiler doesn't throw an error - it simply removes that template from consideration and tries other alternatives.

This template enables conditional compilation:
Primary template (B=false): No 'type' member exists, causing substitution failure
Specialization (B=true): Provides 'type' member, allowing substitution to succeed
